summary.intent: Fix status inheritance for open items across checkpoints so that rephrased items (not just identical text) still carry over their prior status.
summary.outcome: Implemented fuzzy matching using Jaccard similarity (word-overlap) in indexer.ts with a 0.5 threshold, falling back from exact match to best fuzzy match when fetching prior item statuses.

repo: Prior item status lookup previously used exact text matching via `text: { in: sm.open_items }` Prisma query — any rephrasing broke inheritance.
The gossamer project uses Prisma ORM for database queries in src/indexer.ts.
Status inheritance is scoped per session; all non-open prior items for a session are now fetched and matched in-memory.

code.finding: Added findBestPrior(text) function: tries exact match first, then computes Jaccard similarity (case-insensitive, punctuation-stripped tokenization) against all prior non-open items, returning the best match above 0.5 threshold.

workflow: Build verification (TypeScript compile check) was run immediately after editing to confirm no type errors before considering the task complete.

open_items: The 0.5 Jaccard threshold is a heuristic — may need tuning if false positives or false negatives are observed in practice.
prisma/schema.prisma was listed as modified but no schema changes were described; worth verifying what changed there.
